summary: sensivity ships obfuscated payload, persistence, browser scraping, and game-cheat native binary
details: On require/start, launcher.js reads the sibling server.obf.js (a ~150KB obfuscator.io RC4 string-array bundle) and eval()s it in the launcher's scope, so importing the package executes the entire hidden server. The same load path installs an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run autostart entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' that points wscript.exe at a shipped OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs, which silently relaunches `node.exe launcher.js` at every Windows logon — Microsoft-OneDrive impersonation for persistence. A detached background supervisor sets process.title to 'Runtime Broker' (a legitimate Windows OS process name) with windowsHide:true to hide from Task Manager. checkYouTube() spawns PowerShell with UIAutomationClient to walk address-bar controls of every running chrome/msedge/opera/opera_gx/brave process to detect a hardcoded YouTube video ID — unconsented inspection of the user's open browser tabs. checkLicense() (in the obfuscated bundle) builds an HWID by SHA256 of HKLM Cryptography MachineGuid + hostname + C: volume serial and POSTs {key,hwid,nonce,app,version} via fetch to a LICENSE_URL whose host is reconstructed from RC4-decoded fragments (begins 'https://iz...'). The package additionally drops sens.node, an unsigned 6.88 MB Windows PE whose strings (`Enable FL`, `Freecam`, `Teleport`, `spawnVehicle`, `aimbot`/`triggerbot`/`ESP`) and the bundled public/index.html UI identify it as a FiveM/GTA-V game cheat module loaded into the user's Node process via require('./sens.node'). The package's npm description is only 'Sensivity Control Panel' with no homepage or vendor identity.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
